scope: Add BlockRecoveryBenchmark as stable semantic evidence for disk driver recovery timing
Bind recovery evidence to exact-generation BlockDriverCleanup, IoCleanup, VirtioBlkBackend, BlockDevice, DriverStore, Device, and DriverStoreBinding refs
Cross-check cleanup effect counts against completed BlockDriverCleanup evidence
Reject stale cleanup generations and recovery budget overruns before mutation
Expose BlockRecoveryBenchmark roots/counts through semantic package, osctl ViewV1, graph history, replay roots, inspect JSON, direct list JSON, and command evidence
no_goals: real disk scheduler or calibrated recovery timing
adapter-local disk state as semantic truth

findings_fixed: BlockRecoveryBenchmark is historical evidence only and has no live graph ownership edges
Recovery timing is validated against a nonzero budget and rejects overrun before mutation
Cleanup effect counts are cross-checked against completed BlockDriverCleanup evidence
Recovery references use exact generations for cleanup, IO cleanup, backend, block device, driver store, device, and driver binding
Root/count validation covers block_recovery_benchmarks so package tampering is rejected
Stale cleanup generations and budget overruns are rejected before mutation
osctl summary, replay, graph, and stable ViewV1 expose recovery benchmark evidence
bad_smells_checked: No virtio-drivers internals were introduced into semantic_core
No adapter-local timing state is used as semantic truth
The new graph module is domain-scoped instead of expanding block cleanup or target executor logic
Target executor evidence remains a host-side semantic harness and does not run real target cwasm or hardware disk IO
residual_notes: real virtio-blk datapath remains adapter/backend work after semantic contract coverage
Final experiment matrix M7 owns calibrated disk recovery time reporting

residual_gaps: real virtio-blk datapath remains adapter/backend work after semantic contract coverage
Final experiment matrix M7 owns calibrated disk recovery time reporting
